seoTitle: Mount Hunter vs Mount Irvine — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Hunter and Mount Irvine. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Hunter vs Mount Irvine: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Hunter and Mount Irvine.
heading: Mount Hunter vs Mount Irvine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Hunter, New South Wales and Mount Irvine, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Irvine has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Mount Hunter (48/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Hunter or Mount Irvine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Hunter has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Mount Irvine scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Hunter scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Irvine scores 49/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Hunter or Mount Irvine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Irvine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.72% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
